## Review

_Congratulations — you've completed "Academic Writing with Copilot: Co-evolve Your Research Judgment"!_ :tada:

<img src="https://octodex.github.com/images/labtocat.png" alt="celebrate" width="300" align="right">

### What you accomplished

You didn't just produce text — you built a **repeatable research writing workflow** grounded in critical judgment:

- **Defined** a focused research question, hypothesis, and key claims before involving AI.
- **Drafted** with AI assistance and wrote a structured reflection identifying what the AI got right, wrong, and what you changed.
- **Structured** an annotated argument outline, making your reasoning transparent at every section.
- **Reviewed** your full portfolio before merging, applying your judgment as the final quality gate.

### The core skill you've developed

> _AI can accelerate writing. Only you can accelerate thinking._

Every time you reflect on an AI suggestion — accepting, rejecting, or improving it — you are training your own judgment. Over time, that judgment becomes faster, sharper, and harder to fool. That is the compounding return of co-evolving with AI rather than delegating to it.

### What's next?

- Apply this workflow to your next actual paper. Start with `research-question.md` every time.
- Share your `draft-with-reflection.md` with a collaborator or advisor as a transparent record of how you used AI.
- Explore GitHub Copilot's inline suggestions in your editor to speed up the drafting step.
- Try the exercise again with a different paper or research question to see how your reflections evolve.

### Resources

- [GitHub Copilot for research and writing](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot)
- [Communicating using Markdown](https://github.com/skills/communicate-using-markdown) — GitHub Skills exercise
- [Introduction to GitHub](https://github.com/skills/introduction-to-github) — GitHub Skills exercise
